operating_state: Total revenues increased 5.1% to $681.0 billion driven by 5.0% net sales growth across all segments. U.S. comparable sales rose 4.8% led by transaction and unit volume growth in grocery and health and wellness, with eCommerce contributing 2.9% to Walmart U.S. and 2.3% to Sam's Club growth through store-fulfilled pickup and delivery. International segment delivered 6.3% net sales growth despite $3.2 billion negative currency impact, while Sam's Club achieved 4.7% comparable sales growth.
cost_structure: Gross profit margin expanded 40 basis points to 24.1% primarily from Walmart U.S. managing competitive price gaps and higher-margin business growth, partially offset by merchandise mix shifts to lower-margin categories. Operating expenses increased 36 basis points to 20.7% of sales driven by higher variable compensation from exceeding performance targets, increased marketing spend, and elevated depreciation expenses. Operating income margin improved 15 basis points to 4.4% supported by strong membership income growth globally.
material_events: Completed VIZIO acquisition for $1.9 billion net consideration and sold JD.com investment generating $3.6 billion proceeds. Repurchased $4.5 billion of shares under the $20.0 billion authorization program with $12.0 billion remaining capacity. Increased annual dividend to $0.94 per share for fiscal 2026 from $0.83 per share. CEO McMillon established new Rule 10b5-1 trading plan to sell maximum 233,000 shares from June 2025 through May 2026.
macro_exposures: Continued uncertainty from inflationary trends, tariffs and trade restrictions, global currency fluctuations, and supply chain pressures impacting business operations and consumer confidence. Currency exchange rate volatility negatively affected international operations by $3.2 billion in fiscal 2025. Employment trend volatility and macroeconomic swings affecting consumer behavior remain ongoing concerns.
forward_outlook: Projected capital expenditures of $21-25 billion for fiscal 2026 focused on technology, supply chain, and customer-facing initiatives supporting omni-channel growth strategy. Strategic priorities include accelerating eCommerce expansion, increasing Sam's Club and Walmart+ membership penetration, and expanding higher-margin ecosystem businesses including digital advertising and marketplace. Management targeting operating income leverage through productivity improvements and business mix optimization.
uncertainty_profile: Effective tax rate volatility from investment fair value changes and geographic earnings mix fluctuations. Pending accounting standard changes referenced generically without material near-term impact disclosure. Working capital deficit of $17.1 billion managed through efficient cash utilization and consistent capital market access. Repatriation restrictions on $3.3 billion international cash balances subject to local laws and noncontrolling interest approvals.
